text.title: graphql-api Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for graphql-api as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Caches results from one request and serves them to subsequent requests, leaking data between users. Per-request instantiation is mandatory.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: subscriptions-transport-ws is unmaintained and has known issues. The current standard is graphql-ws. Many server libraries still default to the old protocol — opt in explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Input types with nested optional objects creating impossible validation shapes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deeply nested optional inputs produce 2^n possible shapes that are impossible to validate exhaustively. Use flat, single-purpose input types: CreateOrderInput, UpdateOrderStatusInput.

failure-mode.description: Adding a new resolver field without an accompanying DataLoader for 1:N traversal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every parent object triggers a separate DB query. 100 user nodes in a list query = 100 separate order queries. Always require an XByYLoader for any 1:N field.

failure-mode.description: Mixing schema-first and code-first approaches in the same service without documentation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Codegen output drift between the two approaches causes type mismatches that surface only at runtime. Pick one approach and document it in CLAUDE.md.
